title_en: What Indians are reading, and what that reveals about the self
title_zh: 印度人正在读什么，以及这透露了怎样的“自我”
rubric_en: English-language books offer a glimpse of India’s hopes and dreams
rubric_zh: 英文书籍让人一窥印度人的希望与梦想

paragraphs.en: India IS A rare country where hawkers still peddle pirated books at traffic lights. Congestion is often terrible, it is true, but it is not novel-length bad. The black-market trade says several things about the place: that there are not enough bookshops; that Indians are keen readers; and that copyright enforcement is somewhat lax. No matter. For many authors—if not their publishers—seeing knock-off versions of their books from car windows is the ultimate sign of success.
paragraphs.zh: 印度是一个罕见的国家，连红绿灯前的小贩仍在兜售盗版书。交通拥堵常常严重，诚然，但这并不是什么新鲜事。这个地下黑市生意说明了这块土地的几件事：书店数量不足；印度人是热爱阅读的；版权执法也不算严格。无论如何，对许多作者——即便不是他们的出版方——来说，在汽车窗外看到自己作品的山寨版本，反而是最理想的成功象征。

paragraphs.en: The observant commuter will spot something else. In a country where only a small minority has any facility with the language, and an infinitesimally tiny one speaks it natively, all the books sold at red lights are in English. So are most of those at roadside second-hand-book dealers, in bookshops and in the top 100 on Amazon India. English-language books make up about half of (non-educational) official sales. For a country as linguistically and culturally kaleidoscopic as India, that makes English books a rare example of common ground. What Indians read offers a glimpse of their hopes and dreams.
paragraphs.zh: 留意观察的通勤者会发现另一点：在这样一个只有极少数人能熟练使用英语、而母语者又微乎其微的国家，红绿灯前出售的所有书几乎都是英文。路边旧书摊、书店里的畅销书，以及亚马逊印度前100名书单里的书也大多是英文。英文图书约占官方（非教育类）销售量的一半。对于一个语言与文化极其多元、变幻如万花筒般的国家而言，英文书籍成了难得的共同语言。印度人读什么，正好让人一窥他们的希望与梦想。

paragraphs.en: Bookshop displays and bestseller lists reveal the chief concern of the Indian reader to be self-improvement. English is itself the biggest giveaway: it is the language of aspiration, not of pleasure. The split within English is illuminating, too. Half of all books sold are non-fiction, with fiction and children’s books roughly equal. That is in contrast to other big English-language markets, where fiction has a clear lead.
paragraphs.zh: 书店陈列和畅销书榜显示，印度读者最关心的核心议题是自我提升。英语本身就是最醒目的证据：它是一种“志向之语”，而不是“消遣之语”。在英语书籍内部的结构中，这一点也很有启发性：一半以上的书是非虚构类，小说和儿童书大致持平。这与其他大型英文市场形成对比，那些市场通常是小说占据明显优势。

paragraphs.en: That is why “Atomic Habits” and “The Psychology of Money” are reliable bestsellers. “Word Power Made Easy”, which combines linguistic aspiration with the more general kind, is a perennial favourite. The desire for personal growth is not new. “How to Win Friends and Influence People” has been a regular on Indian bookshelves for decades. But it has recently been Indianised. “Do Epic Shit” by Ankur Warikoo, an entrepreneur and content creator (what else?), has sold over 300,000 copies since it came out in 2021. Its secret is to locate its epigrams in modern Indian society. The shops are full of similar books offering advice on life, work, health and relationships written by everyone from celebrities to doctors.
paragraphs.zh: 这也是《原子习惯》和《金钱心理学》成为稳定畅销书的原因。《轻松掌握单词力量》把语言层面的进取心与更广义的求知愿望结合在一起，长期以来一直是最受欢迎的作品。对个人成长的渴望并不新鲜，《人性的弱点》几十年来一直稳居印度书架。只是近来它也被“印度化”了。企业家兼内容创作者安库尔·瓦里库（Ankur Warikoo）所著的《做点令人难忘的大事》（原名《Do Epic Shit》）自2021年出版以来，累计售出超过30万册。它的秘诀在于将书中的格言与当代印度社会紧密对接。书店里满是类似读物，内容涵盖人生、工作、健康与关系，作者从名人到医生应有尽有。

paragraphs.en: Books reveal, too, that Indians are on a journey of self-discovery. Histories of India, often written by foreigners, have always sold well. But now Indians are not just writing their own stories, as Manu Pillai does in his mammoth “Gods, Guns and Missionaries”. They are also interrogating who actually tells these stories, as Sowmiya Ashok does in “The Dig”, a book about “the politics of the past”. Contemporary histories are thickening the genre. Publishers rush out quick-response editions about still-fresh events; a book about last year’s war with Pakistan was out within months of the fighting. Even memoirs by retired bureaucrats and politicians find a market (though mostly among their own friends and contacts).
paragraphs.zh: 书籍也表明，印度人在经历一场自我探索的旅程。关于印度的历史常常由外国人写成，一直卖得很好。但如今，印度人不仅在书写自己的故事，就像 Manu Pillai 在其巨著《上帝、枪炮与传教士》中所做的那样；他们也在追问：究竟是谁在讲述这些故事，正如 Sowmiya Ashok 在《挖掘》中所探讨的“过去的政治”一样。现代历史类图书正在让这一类型更加厚实。出版社会快速推出关于尚未远去事件的应急版；去年与巴基斯坦的战争结束几个月后，一本相关书就已面世。甚至退休官员和政治家的回忆录也能找到市场（尽管主要是在他们自己的朋友和关系网内流通）。

paragraphs.en: Books related to religion, mythology and spirituality are a growing force. One of last year’s bestsellers was a small, inexpensive edition of the “Hanuman Chalisa”, a five-century-old Hindu devotional poem. Gaur Gopal Das, who calls himself an “urban online monk”, writes blockbuster hits that combine self-help with spirituality. India’s foreign minister wrote one about geopolitics based on the lessons of the Ramayana. Other books tell CEOs what they can learn from the Mahabharata.
paragraphs.zh: 与宗教、神话与灵性相关的图书正在成为一股上升力量。去年的畅销书之一是一本袖珍且价格不高的《哈努曼恰利萨》版本，这是一首有着五百年历史的印度教颂歌。自称“都市网禅师”的 Gaur Gopal Das 写出了多部结合了自我帮助与灵性的畅销作品。印度外交部长还写过一本基于《罗摩衍那》启示的地缘政治书籍。其他著作则告诉企业高管可以从《摩诃婆罗多》中汲取什么样的启示。

paragraphs.en: Back at the traffic lights, an idling driver might notice that many bored commuters are whiling away their time on their phones. Indeed. Social media and books share a symbiotic relationship. YouTube videos from literary festivals, Instagram poses from bookshops, photos of annotated pages of Dostoevsky—books feed the feed. And vice versa. Mr Warikoo’s guide was essentially a collection of his social-media posts. Last year’s biggest fiction hit was “Too Good to Be True”, a romance novel whose author, Prajakta Koli, is a YouTuber with millions of subscribers. Books reveal the Indian reader—both creator and consumer—to be versed in the art of self-promotion.
paragraphs.zh: 回到十字路口的红绿灯前，发呆的司机也许会注意到，许多无聊的通勤者正把时间消磨在手机上。确实如此。社交媒体与图书之间存在一种共生关系。文学节的 YouTube 视频、书店里的 Instagram 摆拍、托尔斯泰（陀思妥耶夫斯基）批注页的照片——都是“书”在喂“动态”。反过来亦然。Warikoo 先生的指南几乎就是他社交媒体帖子的合集。去年的最大畅销小说是《太美好以至于不真实》——一部浪漫小说，其作者 Prajakta Koli 是一位拥有数百万订阅者的 YouTuber。书籍揭示出印度读者——既是创作者也是消费者——熟稔自我营销的艺术。

paragraphs.en: Yet social media does not transform the act of reading. It is at heart a solo endeavour. In India it is also a socially acceptable way of cultivating an individual identity in what remains a deeply collectivist society. Whether undertaken in pursuit of self-improvement, self-discovery or self-promotion, its appeal points to a deeper aspiration of modern Indians: the desire for self-expression. ■
paragraphs.zh: 然而社交媒体并不会改变阅读这一行为本身。阅读在核心上仍是一项个人活动。在印度，它也是在依旧深受集体主义影响的社会中，培育个人身份的一种被社会认可的方式。无论是为了自我提升、自我探索还是自我包装，它的吸引力都指向现代印度人更深层的愿望：对自我表达的渴求。■

paragraphs.en: Subscribers to The Economist can sign up to our Opinion newsletter, which brings together the best of our leaders, columns, guest essays and reader correspondence.
paragraphs.zh: 《经济学人》的订阅者可以订阅我们的《观点》通讯，该栏目汇集了我们最优秀的社论、专栏、客座文章和读者来信。
